name: Pumpkin Walnut Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Cooked long-grain rice (white or brown)
recipeInstructions:
"In season, use fresh, small pumpkins; out of season use canned."
Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit. Spray a large non-stick skillet with nonstick cooking spray. Cook onion and bell peppers over medium heat, stirring often, until onion is pale gold, about 5 minutes.
Add pumpkin and broth, cover and cook over low heat until pumpkin is soft, about 7 minutes. Remove from heat; add rice, breadcrumbs, walnuts, egg, parsley, salt, sage, and pepper; mix well.
Spray a 4-cup baking dish with nonstick cooking spray. Pack rice mixture into dish; sprinkle with Parmesan and bake for 25 minutes. Run under broiler for 1-2 minutes until lightly browned on top.
Meanwhile, in a small saucepan, bring the stewed tomatoes to a boil over high heat, stirring frequently. Cook, uncovered, until reduced to 1 cup.
Serve as is or puree in blender or food processor. Serve loaf with sauce on the side.
Each serving (1/4 loaf with 1/4 cup sauce) provides:
1 Fat, 2 Vitamins, 1 Bone, 1 Carbohydrate
Per serving:
299 calories, 10 grams protein, 38 grams carbohydrates
13 grams fat: 6 grams polyunsaturated, 3 grams monounsaturated, 2 grams saturated
362 milligrams sodium, 57 milligrams cholesterol
